messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following telemedicine platform privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"MediConnect processes your name, date of birth, medical history, prescriptions, and video consultation recordings to deliver telemedicine services. We obtain explicit written consent before processing any health data. You may withdraw consent at any time by contacting us, after which we will cease processing but may retain records for 10 years as required by medical regulations. You have the right to access, correct, restrict, port, and erase your data, and to object to processing. Our DPO is reachable at dpo@mediconnect.eu. All data is stored in certified data centres in the Netherlands. We notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours of a confirmed breach. Our sub-processors are listed at mediconnect.eu/processors."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Explicit consent stated for health data processing, satisfying Art. 6(1)(a). Regulatory retention invokes Art. 6(1)(c).

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Health data correctly identified as special category. Explicit consent obtained per Art. 9(2)(a). Medical regulatory retention covered by Art. 9(2)(h).

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Purpose clearly stated: delivery of telemedicine services. Retention after consent withdrawal is separately justified by medical regulation.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate and necessary for telemedicine delivery.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 10-year retention post-consent withdrawal is stated and grounded in medical regulatory requirements.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) are all explicitly listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Sub-processors listed at a dedicated URL, satisfying Art. 28 transparency requirements.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] All data stored in the Netherlands confirms EEA-based processing. No transfer concerns.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact disclosed at dpo@mediconnect.eu.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour supervisory authority notification stated.

VERDICT: Compliant
VIOLATIONS: None
RECOMMENDATIONS: No changes required. Consider specifying the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (Art. 77) as an additional transparency measure.
